LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE BAY AREA CENTER FOR ORAL & MAXILLOIFACIAL AND JUAN LUQUE, DDS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED. BASED ON THE DECLARATIONS OF TANIA NELSON-CHRYSTAL AND GARY SCHOOLNIK, THERE ARE TRIABLE DISPUTES WHETHER DR. LUQUE ACTED WITHIN THE STANDARD OF CARE IN HIS DIAGNOSIS, TREATMENT, AND POST-SURGICAL CARE OF PLAINTIFF ADAM BRODNER WITH RESPECT TO THE EXTRACTION OF TOOTH 32 AND MR. BRODNER'S COMPLAINTS OF PAIN AND OTHER MATTERS AFTER THE EXTRACTION OF TEETH 17 AND 32. DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATIONS OF DR. NELSON-CHYSTAL AND DR. SCHOOLNIK ARE OVERRULED. THERE IS SUFFICIENT FOUNDATION FOR THEIR OPINIONS AND THOSE OPINIONS ARE NOT BASED ON SPECULATION. MOST OR ALL OF THE OBJECTIONS GO THE WEIGHT, NOT THE ADMISSIBILITY, OF THE OPINIONS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 AND C.C.P. SECTION 437(C)(G). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-09-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-08-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-06-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
Off Calendar Nov-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Mark J. Zanobini of the law offices of Mark J. Zanobini (415-392-8400) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jenny A. Silverstein of the law offices of Tyson & Mendes (628-253-5066) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS THE BAY AREA CENTER FOR ORAL & MAXILLOIFACIAL and JUAN LUQUE, DDS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Based on the declarations of Tania Nelson-Chrystal and Gary Schoolnik, there are triable disputes whether Dr. Luque acted within the standard of care in his diagnosis, treatment, and post-surgical care of plaintiff Adam Brodner with respect to the extraction of tooth 32 and Mr. Brodner's complaints of pain and other matters after the extraction of teeth 17 and 32. Defendants' objections to the declarations of Dr. Nelson-Chystal and Dr. Schoolnik are overruled. There is sufficient foundation for their opinions and those opinions are not based on speculation. Most or all of the objections go the weight, not the admissibility, of the opinions. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312 and C.C.P. Section 437(c)(g).

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)
